title: Contact Manager - Final Touch & Future Scope

content.problemStatement:
reat! You have successfully built a **Contact Manager** using Java. 🎉

Here are some improvements you can try:

- **Save Contacts to a File** – Store contacts in a text or CSV file to retain them after restarting the program.
- **Load Contacts from a File** – Automatically read and load existing contacts when the program starts.
- **Enhanced Search** – Allow searching with partial names or phone numbers (case-insensitive).
- **User Interface** – Add a simple GUI using Java Swing or JavaFX.

### **Final Updates:**
- Ensure all functions (Add, View, Search, Update, Delete) work properly.
- Handle invalid inputs with appropriate error messages.
- Improve user experience with clearer prompts and cleaner formatting.

Try implementing these improvements to make your Contact Manager even more robust and user-friendly! 🚀
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
